# DejaDB

> An embedded memory engine for AI agents — memory that doesn't rot, stays current, and proves where every fact came from. Store memories as immutable, content-addressed grains in a per-memory file, query them with CAL, and render model-ready context in-process — no server in the recall path. The file format is documented and OMS-conformant, so your memory outlives the engine.

DejaDB is a Rust workspace (with a `deja` CLI, an MCP server, and Python and
Node bindings) built on Turso, a SQLite-compatible engine. Memories are immutable and
content-addressed (SHA-256 over each `.mg` blob): every edit is a supersession
and every removal a tombstone, giving git-like history, forks with explicit
merges, and provenance. Recall is hybrid — structural, BM25, and optional vector
legs fused with Reciprocal Rank Fusion — and CAL, the query language, is
structurally incapable of deleting data (`DELETE`/`DROP` are not grammar tokens).
DejaDB takes no LLM dependency: extraction and embeddings are supplied by the
host. It is pre-1.0 (`0.1.x`) — usable and tested, with APIs still stabilizing.

## Docs

- [README](README.md): Project overview, quickstart (CLI, Python, MCP), benchmarks, and the crate map.
- [Architecture](ARCHITECTURE.md): The 9-crate workspace, the grain model, the store schema, hybrid recall, and the sync/forks design.
- [CAL reference](docs/cal-reference.md): The Context Assembly Language — `RECALL`, `ASSEMBLE`, `EXISTS`, `HISTORY`, `ADD`, `SUPERSEDE`, pipelines, and the non-destructive guarantee.
- [MCP reference](docs/mcp-reference.md): The 6 stdio MCP tools (`dejadb_recall/add/supersede/forget/remember/cal`) and how to wire them into an MCP client.
- [Migration guide](docs/migrate.md): `deja migrate` — importing from mem0 (with full edit history), Zep/Graphiti, Letta, LangMem/LangGraph, Basic Memory, or generic JSONL, with per-source export one-liners.
- [Memory-tool backend](docs/memory-tool.md): DejaDB as the storage backend for Anthropic's client-side memory tool (`memory_20250818`) in Python, Node, and the CLI.
- [FAQ](FAQ.md): Question-and-answer coverage of what DejaDB is, how it differs from vector DBs and RAG, grains, OMS, recall, encryption, and maturity.
- [Cookbook](docs/cookbook.md): Copy-pasteable recipes — add/recall, CAL queries, running the MCP server, encryption at rest, backup/restore, streaming/sync, Python, the web console, and building a self-improving agent (experience log → distilled lessons → proficiency chain → rollback).

## Operations & contributing

- [Security policy](SECURITY.md): The trust boundary, encryption-at-rest caveats, known limitations, disclosure process, and an operator hardening checklist.
- [Contributing](CONTRIBUTING.md): DCO sign-off, the invariants you must not break, the "don't run blanket cargo fmt" rule, and the PR flow.
- [Error codes](ERROR_CODES.md): The append-only `DOMAIN-Ennn` registry (`FMT/MEM/STO/CRY/VAL/CAL/SYS`).

## Optional

- [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md): Orientation for an AI coding agent working inside the DejaDB repo itself (build/test, invariants, where things live).
- [Changelog](CHANGELOG.md): What exists in the current release candidate.
- [Open Memory Spec](https://github.com/openmemoryspec/oms): The external CC0 specification DejaDB implements.
